title: Misanteca cayennensis
intro: Misanteca cayennensis, also known as West Indian bay or pimento, is a tropical evergreen tree native to the Caribbean and Central America. The plant is valued for its aromatic leaves, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Misanteca cayennensis include essential oils, particularly eugenol and chavicol, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the leaves of Misanteca cayennensis are commonly prepared as a tea, infused in oil, or made into a syrup to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Misanteca cayennensis is a species of plant that belongs to the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard apple family. This family consists of approximately 163 genera and over 2,300 species of flowering plants. Misanteca cayennensis is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant's classification is further detailed within the genus Misanteca, which comprises several species of flowering plants. Misanteca cayennensis is a small tree or shrub that is often found growing in moist, tropical environments.

parts: Misanteca cayennensis is also known as West Indian bay or West Indian laurel.
